sense.definition: (in sports) the players of a team who are trying to score against the rival team
(in sports) the players who try to not allow the opposing team to score; the position or role of these players on the field
a versatile player in soccer or American football positioned between the forwards and defenders, responsible for both offensive and defensive duties

examples.x: The halfback's role in soccer involves linking the defense with the midfield and aiding in ball distribution.
In American football, the halfback is pivotal for running with the ball, catching passes, and blocking.
Coaches rely on the halfback to maintain possession and create scoring opportunities.
Playing as a halfback requires understanding the flow of the game and adapting to changing situations.
The halfback needs to be agile and have good decision-making skills under pressure.

sense.definition: a pitcher who comes into the game from the bullpen to replace another pitcher

examples.x: The reliever struck out the last batter to end the inning.
The reliever entered the game in the eighth inning.
The reliever pitched a scoreless inning.
The manager called for a left-handed reliever to face the upcoming batters.
The closer is their best reliever for finishing games.

sense.definition: (baseball) a pitcher who enters the game after the starting pitcher but before the closer

examples.x: The team relied on their middle reliever to hold the lead in the seventh inning.
He transitioned from a starting role to become a reliable middle reliever for the bullpen.
The middle reliever struggled with his command, walking two batters in a row.
The manager brought in the middle reliever to face the left-handed hitters in the lineup.
The middle reliever warmed up quickly when the starter began to tire in the fifth inning.

sense.definition: a player who operates primarily in the central area of the field in sports like soccer or rugby, with both defensive and offensive roles

examples.x: The midfielder intercepted the pass and quickly launched a counterattack.
The midfielder threaded a perfect through-ball to the striker, who scored easily.
Our midfielders need to work on their tackling to strengthen our defensive line.
The coach praised the midfielder's work rate and tactical awareness.
Midfielders often act as the link between the defense and the forwards.

sense.definition: (baseball) a pitcher who enters a game in relief of the starting pitcher and typically pitches multiple innings

examples.x: The long reliever came in during the fifth inning to stabilize the game.
The long reliever pitched three scoreless innings to earn the win.
His performance as a long reliever has earned him recognition throughout the league.
He's known as one of the team's most reliable long relievers.
The manager called on the long reliever after the starter struggled in the third inning.

sense.definition: (baseball) a relief pitcher who precedes the closer, usually pitching in the eighth inning to maintain the team's lead

examples.x: The setup man came in to pitch the eighth inning with a one-run lead.
He's been the team's setup man all season, setting up numerous save opportunities for the closer.
The manager trusts him as the setup man because of his consistent performance in late-game situations.
The setup man got the job done again, retiring the side without allowing any runs.
In baseball, the setup man's role is crucial in bridging the gap between the starting pitcher and the closer.

sense.definition: a player in baseball, who excels in facing or pitching against left-handed opponents

examples.x: The team brought in a left-handed specialist to face the dangerous lefty hitters in the opposing lineup.
As a left-handed specialist, his job was to disrupt the rhythm of left-handed hitters with his unique pitching style.
She became known as a left-handed specialist for her ability to consistently hit against right-handed pitchers.
The manager called upon the left-handed specialist to neutralize the opponent's top left-handed batter.
His career flourished when he embraced his role as a left-handed specialist in the later innings of games.

sense.definition: a versatile player who can play effectively at multiple positions, typically in basketball or baseball

examples.x: He proved to be a valuable swingman, seamlessly transitioning from defense to offense.
The coach relies on the swingman to provide flexibility and adaptability on the court.
The basketball team's swingman excels both as a shooting guard and a small forward.
As a swingman, his ability to defend multiple positions makes him a key asset to the team.
The team drafted a promising young swingman known for his athleticism and scoring ability.

sense.definition: (cricket) a player who throws the ball to the batsman
(cricket) the player who stands behind the stumps to catch deliveries from the bowler and attempt to dismiss batsmen

examples.x: The wicket-keeper missed a stumping opportunity.
The wicket-keeper shouted instructions to the fielders.
She's known for her quick reflexes as a wicket-keeper.
The wicket-keeper signaled for a change in fielding positions.
The wicket-keeper wears gloves and stands close to the stumps.

sense.definition: a player on the cricket team who positions themselves strategically to stop or catch the ball hit by the batsman, aiming to dismiss them or prevent runs

examples.x: The fielder took a diving catch at point to dismiss the batsman.
He's a brilliant fielder, known for his athleticism and quick reflexes in the outfield.
The fielder at cover stopped the ball with a dive to prevent a boundary.
Positioned at point, the fielder chased down the ball and returned it swiftly to the bowler.
Standing at fine leg, the fielder's swift fielding prevented the ball from crossing the boundary.

sense.definition: a player who is currently advancing or attempting to advance along the bases after hitting the ball

examples.x: The speedy runner stole second base.
The runner on third base tagged up and scored on a sacrifice fly.
The leadoff runner scored the winning run in the ninth inning.
The runner slid into home plate to avoid the tag and score.
The runner rounded second base aggressively and headed for third.

sense.definition: a soccer player who operates primarily on the flanks of the field, contributing both defensively and offensively

examples.x: The team's wide midfielder delivered a pinpoint cross into the box for the striker to score.
She excels as a wide midfielder due to her speed and ability to take on defenders.
The coach instructed the wide midfielder to track back quickly to support the defense.
The opposing team struggled to contain our quick and agile wide midfielder.
In modern soccer tactics, the wide midfielder often plays a crucial role in providing width to the attack.

sense.definition: an American football player who specializes in punting the ball to the opposing team, usually on fourth down, to change field position

examples.x: He's the team's punter and also handles kickoffs.
The punter's kick was high and allowed the coverage team to tackle the returner quickly.
He's the team's punter and also handles kickoffs.
Mastering hang time, the punter disrupts the return game with each kick.
The punter angled his kick toward the sideline to pin the opponents deep in their own territory.

sense.definition: the palyer that is judged to be the most significant or useful in the team
a player who specializes in kicking the ball, usually for field goals, extra points, and kickoffs

examples.x: The kicker nailed a 50-yard field goal to win the game.
He's the team's reliable kicker, known for his accuracy under pressure.
With seconds left on the clock, the kicker lined up for a game-winning field goal attempt.
The kicker's extra point was good, extending the team's lead to seven points.
The rookie kicker made his NFL debut with a successful 40-yard field goal.

sense.definition: (baseball) a baserunner on base when a relief pitcher enters the game, with the responsibility of the previous pitcher

examples.x: The closer faced a tough situation with an inherited runner on third base.
He struggled to strand inherited runners, often allowing them to score.
The manager brought in a left-handed pitcher to handle the inherited runner.
She was known for her ability to minimize damage with inherited runners.
The relief pitcher successfully stranded all inherited runners last season.

sense.definition: a player in cricket who specializes in bowling spin deliveries

examples.x: The young spinner made his debut in international cricket last year.
The team's spinner took five wickets in yesterday's match.
The spinner's variations in pace and flight confuse batsmen.
The team is looking to recruit a new spinner for the upcoming season.
The veteran spinner announced his retirement after a successful career.

sense.definition: a player who starts a game or match

examples.x: The opener swiftly scored a goal within the first five minutes of the match.
She's the team's designated opener, known for her aggressive playstyle.
He's a reliable opener for the cricket team, frequently scoring half-centuries.
The volleyball opener's serves were consistently difficult for the opponents to return.
The basketball team's opener dominated the court with his impressive dribbling skills.

sense.definition: a defensive player in sports, typically football or soccer, who plays as both a full back and a winger

examples.x: She excels as a wing back, covering the flank with speed and precision.
In modern tactics, wing backs often contribute significantly to the team's attacking plays.
She adjusted well to her new position as a wing back after transitioning from midfield.
The team's strategy relies on aggressive wing backs for both offensive and defensive support.
The wing back intercepted the pass and initiated a counterattack.

sense.definition: a player in sports such as hockey or soccer who primarily plays on the defensive end of the field or rink

examples.x: The defenseman intercepted the pass and cleared it out of the zone.
The defenseman's role is crucial in preventing the opposing team from scoring.
He transitioned from forward to defenseman after realizing his strength in defensive play.
The defenseman's slap shot from the blue line resulted in a goal for his team.
The defenseman's speed allows him to cover a lot of ground on the ice.

sense.definition: a group of reserve players on a sports team who are available to be called up to the main roster as needed

examples.x: The hockey team's taxi squad includes promising young forwards and defensemen.
Teams often rotate players between the taxi squad and the main roster based on performance and injuries.
The football coach called up two players from the taxi squad for this weekend's game.
The baseball team keeps its taxi squad active during the season to ensure depth in all positions.
Several athletes on the taxi squad have already played at the college level and are eager for their pro debut.

sense.definition: any player on the baseball defensive team who positions themselves in the field to catch or field the ball hit by the batter

examples.x: He's an excellent fielder, known for his strong arm and quick reflexes.
The pitcher is also a skilled fielder, often fielding bunts and grounders.
The fielder positioned himself perfectly to catch the pop fly.
The shortstop is one of the team's best fielders, with a high fielding percentage.
The fielder covered a lot of ground to catch the deep fly ball.

sense.definition: any player who is currently carrying the football during a play

examples.x: The runner followed his blockers and dove into the end zone for a touchdown.
The quarterback handed the ball off to the runner on a sweep to the left.
With a burst of acceleration, the runner turned the corner and raced down the sideline.
The runner's ability to read blocks and find running lanes makes him a valuable asset.
After receiving a pitch from the quarterback, the runner cut back across the field.

sense.definition: the player in charge of a sports team
(cricket) a player skilled in both batting and bowling

examples.x: She emerged as a promising all-rounder after her exceptional performance in the last series.
The team relies on their all-rounder to contribute significantly in every match.
Finding a talented all-rounder is crucial for balancing the team's performance.
He is known as an all-rounder because he excels in both batting and bowling.
It's rare to find an all-rounder who can consistently deliver with both bat and ball.

sense.definition: a player who excels in multiple roles within their sport

examples.x: She's a hybrid player, equally effective as a point guard and shooting guard.
The team's success is due in part to their versatile hybrid players.
His agility and strength make him a valuable hybrid player in both offense and defense.
In basketball, a hybrid player like him can dominate inside the paint and shoot from long range.
The soccer team benefits from having several hybrid players who can play midfield and defense interchangeably.

sense.definition: an American football player who lines up in the line of scrimmage

examples.x: The offensive lineman's quick feet allowed him to protect the quarterback from the pass rush.
The defensive lineman disrupted the play by tackling the running back behind the line of scrimmage.
Overpowering his opponent with a bull rush, the defensive lineman disrupted the play.
The offensive lineman's role is crucial in providing protection to the quarterback during passing plays.
Reacting swiftly to the play, the defensive lineman's instincts make him a standout defender.

sense.definition: a versatile player who can play multiple positions competently

examples.x: The team's utility player filled in at shortstop during the playoffs.
He's known as a utility player because he can pitch and play outfield equally well.
She became a valuable utility player, switching between center and power forward as needed.
The coach relies on him as a utility player to handle various defensive positions.
Being a utility player requires adaptability and a strong understanding of the game.

sense.definition: a volleyball player who primarily attacks from the right side of the court

examples.x: The opposite hitter executed a powerful spike to win the set.
She transitioned from setter to opposite hitter seamlessly this season.
The team relies on their opposite hitter for critical points.
His height makes him a formidable opposite hitter at the net.
The opposite hitter adjusted her approach to counter the block.

sense.definition: a baseball player from the batting team who takes their turn to bat and attempts to score runs by hitting the ball delivered by the bowler

examples.x: The batsman drove the ball through covers for a boundary.
He's a skilled batsman, known for his ability to play both spin and pace.
The aggressive batsman attacked the bowler from the first ball.
The opening batsman faced the first over of the match confidently.
The batsman defended the ball well to avoid getting out.

sense.definition: a female cricket player who bats

examples.x: The batswoman scored a century in yesterday's match.
She is known for being the most skilled batswoman on the team.
The young batswoman showed promising talent in her debut game.
Our school's star batswoman won several awards this season.
As a batswoman, she prefers to play aggressively.

sense.definition: a baseball pitcher who throws very fast pitches

examples.x: He's a fireballer, throwing pitches at over 100 mph.
The team needs a fireballer to strengthen their pitching rotation
The fireballer struck out the side in the last inning.
Scouts are watching the young fireballer closely.
The manager relied on the fireballer to close out crucial games during the season.

sense.definition: (rugby) a player who breaks away from the opposing team's defense, often with the ball

examples.x: She plays as a breakaway, making decisive moves in attack.
John is a skilled breakaway known for his speed and agility.
Our breakaway is crucial in turning defense into attack.
The coach praised the breakaway for their strategic play.
The breakaway seized the opportunity and dashed through the gap in the defense.

sense.definition: a player in rugby union positioned on the side of the scrum, primarily responsible for attacking and defending at the breakdown
(baseball) the catcher, who stands behind home plate and is responsible for catching pitches, preventing passed balls, and managing the defense
a rugby player in the front row of the scrum who uses their feet to hook the ball back to their team
the baseball player who begins the game
(rugby) a forward player who supports the scrum and is responsible for providing power and stability
a relief pitcher who is brought in to pitch the final innings, usually the ninth
a forward in basketball who handles playmaking duties
